assertions.title: Python sidecar IPC protocol — subcommands dispatch correctly

steps.action: Run `tg.exe classify --help` to verify subcommand exists
steps.expected: Help text for classify subcommand displayed
steps.observed: Help text displayed: 'Run semantic NLP threat classification on logs via cyBERT'. Exit code 0.

steps.action: Create sample log file with INFO/ERROR/WARN lines
Run `tg.exe classify sample_test.log` and capture stdout/stderr/exit
Run `python -m tensor_grep classify sample_test.log` and capture stdout/stderr/exit
steps.expected: Same JSON classification output as tg.exe
steps.observed: stdout matches tg.exe byte-for-byte. stderr matches (PyTorch warning). Exit code 0.

steps.expected: Stdout, stderr, and exit codes all match
steps.observed: STDOUT: MATCH. STDERR: MATCH. EXIT CODES: tg=0 py=0 match=True

steps.action: Run cargo test --release --test test_sidecar_ipc (4 integration tests)
steps.expected: All 4 tests pass including stdout equality, large payload stress, crash handling, missing python
steps.observed: test_tg_classify_stdout_matches_python_module ... ok; test_sidecar_protocol_handles_large_payload_and_large_stdout ... ok; test_sidecar_crash_reports_error_without_hanging ... ok; test_missing_python_reports_actionable_error ... ok. 4 passed, 0 failed.

evidence.consoleErrors: none (PyTorch import warning is expected — not an error)
evidence.network: N/A — CLI tool, no network requests

assertions.title: Sidecar crash produces clean error — no hang

steps.action: Create mock crash sidecar script that writes partial JSON then exits 1
steps.observed: mock_sidecar_crash.py created — writes partial JSON '{"stdout":' then raises SystemExit(1)

steps.action: Run `tg.exe classify` with TG_SIDECAR_SCRIPT pointing to crash script
steps.expected: tg.exe exits non-zero within 5s with human-readable error in stderr
steps.observed: Exit code: 1. Completed in 0.13 seconds (well under 30s). stderr: 'Python sidecar exited with code 1 before returning valid JSON: EOF while parsing a value at line 1 column 10'

steps.action: Verify no hang, no panic, no exit 0
steps.expected: Non-zero exit, no Rust panic trace, completes quickly
steps.observed: Exit code 1 (non-zero). No panic trace. Completed in 0.13s. Human-readable error message.

assertions.title: Missing Python sidecar produces actionable error message

steps.action: Copy tg.exe to isolated temp directory (away from .venv)
Run isolated tg.exe classify with PATH='C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows' (no Python)
steps.expected: Exit code 2, stderr contains 'Python sidecar not found', no Rust panic
steps.observed: Exit code: 2. stderr: 'Python sidecar not found. Tried `python`. Set TG_SIDECAR_PYTHON to an interpreter path or create `.venv` with `uv pip install -e ".[dev,ast,nlp]"`.' No Rust panic trace. No raw OS error.

steps.action: Verify error message is user-friendly and actionable
steps.expected: Message identifies problem and provides fix instructions
steps.observed: Message states 'Python sidecar not found', names the attempted interpreter ('python'), and provides two fix options: set TG_SIDECAR_PYTHON env var or create .venv.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. VAL-CTRL-002: tg.exe classify output matches python -m tensor_grep classify byte-for-byte (stdout, stderr, exit code). All 4 cargo sidecar integration tests pass including 2MB stress test. VAL-CTRL-NEW-002: Crashing sidecar produces clean error 'sidecar exited with code 1 before returning valid JSON' within 0.13s, exit code 1, no hang. VAL-CTRL-NEW-003: With sanitized PATH (no Python), tg.exe exits with code 2 and stderr message 'Python sidecar not found' with actionable fix instructions, no Rust panic.
